step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the consensus that the most common site of zygomatic arch fracture is indeed anterior to the zygomaticotemporal suture. One additional point worth noting is that this region often bears the brunt of lateral head-on collisions which frequently result in facial trauma. Since the anterior part of the zygomatic arch tends to encounter greater stress compared to its posterior counterpart, it logically follows that this segment becomes more susceptible to fractures under such circumstances.

Therefore, my answer remains: A.
